---
title: "Result"
author: "Ka Long"
date: "11/17/2021"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r load-library, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
require(knitr)
require(arsenal)
require(readr)
```

```{r, load-data, , echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
dataset2 <- read_csv("dataset2.csv")
dataset2 <- as.data.frame(unclass(dataset2),stringsAsFactors = TRUE)
```

```{r Regression, results='asis', echo = FALSE}
summary(modelsum(list(religion_scale_2_r_attend_r, religion_scale_3_r_pray, religion_scale_4_r_study_r_almost_each_day, religion_scale_4_r_study_r_each_day) ~ religion_scale_1, adjust= ~FACIT_meaning + FACIT_peace + FACIT_faith + IES_intrusion + IES_avoidance + IES_hyperarousal + HADS_anxiety + HADS_depression, data=dataset2, family = binomial, 
                         #binomial.stats=c("OR", "CI.lower.OR", "CI.upper.OR", "p.value", "concordance", "statistic", "logLik", "AIC", "BIC")
                         ), term.name = TRUE)
```
